authority_note: Starter bounded entries. nominal_resistance_multiplier is the only output-driving field per §8.4. All values are operator-estimated representative bounds requiring sourcing before operational use.

entries.label: Direct Cold Plate
entries.source_note: Representative direct cold plate. Multiplier=1.0 means nominal spreader-to-pickup resistance applies directly. Verify TIM resistance and contact area for specific assembly.

entries.label: Dual-Sided Package Pickup
entries.source_note: Two-sided package pickup reduces effective resistance per unit area. Multiplier<1.0 reflects parallel heat path. Representative estimate — requires module-specific sourcing.
entries.notes: Packaging constraint: requires substrate access on both sides.

entries.label: Microchannel Plate Interface
entries.source_note: Microchannel cold plate bonded to package. Higher multiplier than flat plate reflects manifold and bond resistance additions. Representative — fabrication-specific data required.
entries.notes: High heat flux capable. Pressure drop must be sized into loop.

entries.label: Vapor Chamber Interface
entries.source_note: Vapor chamber spreader to cold plate or loop interface. Excellent spreading, adds vapor chamber-to-loop coupling resistance. Representative.
entries.notes: Mass penalty vs. spreading benefit must be traded.

entries.label: Heat Pipe Interface
entries.source_note: Embedded heat pipe saddle-bonded to package. Lower contact fraction and higher effective resistance than plate geometries due to line-contact geometry. Representative.

entries.label: Immersion Bath Pickup
entries.source_note: Full immersion of package in dielectric or compatible fluid. Lowest effective resistance multiplier due to full-surface contact. Compatibility with space environment and long-duration operation research required.
entries.notes: Fluid management in microgravity is unresolved. Research required before mission application.
